note: Strings that yt-dlp surfaces but does NOT originate. They come from external APIs (YouTube player response, Twitch, etc.) and are passed through verbatim. Curated by hand because the scan script can't see them in yt-dlp source. Every entry carries provenance.

strings.fragment: Sign in to confirm you're not a bot
strings.provenance: YouTube playabilityStatus.reason — passed through by yt-dlp in extractor/youtube/_video.py via raise_no_formats
strings.notes: Apostrophe is ASCII U+0027 in some locales/clients

strings.fragment: Sign in to confirm you’re not a bot
strings.provenance: YouTube playabilityStatus.reason — current default uses U+2019
strings.notes: Apostrophe is U+2019 (RIGHT SINGLE QUOTATION MARK)

strings.fragment: This content isn't available, try again later
strings.provenance: YouTube playabilityStatus.reason — expanded by extractor/youtube/_video.py:4055-4060 with 'has been rate-limited by YouTube' suffix

strings.fragment: has been rate-limited by YouTube for up to an hour
strings.provenance: extractor/youtube/_video.py:4057 — appended to the YouTube reason when 'try again later' matches

strings.fragment: The uploader has not made this video available in your country
strings.provenance: YouTube playabilityStatus.subreason — extractor/youtube/_video.py forwards via raise_geo_restricted
YouTube playabilityStatus.reason and test 'skip' fields in extractor/youtube/_video.py

strings.fragment: Your IP is likely being blocked by Youtube
strings.provenance: extractor/youtube/_video.py:3190 — raised when all player responses are invalid

strings.fragment: YouTube is requiring a captcha challenge before playback
strings.provenance: extractor/youtube/_video.py:4053 — appended when playerCaptchaViewModel is present in errorScreen
